data.title: Vertiv: Reinforcing Buy on Strong Data Center Demand, Upgraded 2026 Outlook, and AI-Driven Growth Tailwinds
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Michael Elias has reiterated their bullish stance on VRT stock, giving a Buy rating on April 21.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Michael Elias has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Vertiv’s improving fundamentals and powerful demand backdrop. He notes that the company outperformed expectations in 1Q26 and lifted its 2026 outlook, while management’s remarks suggest that the order pipeline remains strong even after a period of exceptionally high bookings.

Elias also emphasizes that Vertiv’s expanding Americas pipeline matches independent evidence of unprecedented U.S. data center leasing, which he believes can translate into record orders as soon as 2Q26. With an upcoming Investor Day likely to bring higher long‑term targets and data center infrastructure acting as a key beneficiary of sustained AI‑driven demand, he continues to view Vertiv as a top “picks and shovels” way to invest in the theme for 2026.

In another report released on April 21, Bank of America Securities also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $330.00 price target.

data.title: Balanced Risk/Reward Keeps VRNS at Hold Amid Execution Risks and Compressed Valuation
data.description:
Shrenik Kothari, an analyst from Robert W. Baird, maintained the Hold rating on Varonis Systems. The associated price target remains the same with $32.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shrenik Kothari has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to execution risk and a more balanced risk/reward profile. He views management’s guidance around SaaS conversions and ARR as intentionally conservative, noting that assumptions for churn, net retention, deal size, and new business leave room for upside if execution improves, but also underscore that results are highly dependent on operational follow-through.

At the same time, he acknowledges that VRNS shares have already sold off sharply, leaving valuation notably compressed versus historical levels and peers, which limits further downside in his view. However, near-term growth headwinds from tough revenue and ARR comparisons, the lingering impact of prior sales misallocation toward conversions, and ongoing competitive and visibility concerns lead him to see the stock as fairly balanced rather than compellingly mispriced, supporting a Hold stance rather than a more aggressive rating.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
